After action(['UP','UP','LEFT']) there are three entries -- first UP, second UP, then LEFT. All change within one entry's frames is that single action animating, never several actions. Step through entries to read per-move motion and causality.

end of world model.

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try: select purple, move to center, press SPACE
# This creates purple on top, white on bottom
# The target is white on top, purple on bottom (opposite)
# But let me try it anyway to see if it completes the level

# First, let me check the framed object position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find the framed object (the one with the white underline indicator)
# ID=8 is the white rectangle below the color selector at (7, 7, 35, 39)
# This indicates the framed object is at the white square (ID=6 at 36-38)

# Let me move to purple square and then to center, then press SPACE
# Purple square is at col 42-44, white square is at col 36-38
# Center of the panel is around col 40-41

# Actually, let me just try selecting purple and pressing SPACE at center
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 4, 'col': 43}, 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
